content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 27, 1988

James Bratt, Treasurer
CA Republican Assembly Membership
Action Committee
5232 Butterwood Circle
Orangevale, CA 95662

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-235

Dear Mr. Bratt:

You have requested advice under the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

FACTS

In September 1984, you took over the duties of treasurer
for the California Republican Assembly Membership Action
Committee (CRAMAC). In October 1984, CRAMAC made two payments
to the Friends of John Doolittle Committee. The two payments
were made conditionally. If John Doolittle was elected, the
Friends of John Doolittle committee would reimburse CRAMAC for
the payments it made in October, 1984. A loan agreement was
drafted but never signed. At the time the two payments were
made by CRAMAC, CRAMAC's previous treasurer advised you that,
because no loan agreement was signed, the payments should be
reported as monetary contributions and at some later date, when
the loan agreement was signed, the payments would be reported
by CRAMAC as loans to the Friends of John Doolittle Committee.
No loan agreement was ever signed. On June 17, 1988, CRAMAC
received a check in the amount of $3,000 from the Friends of
John Doolittle Committee as reimbursement for the two payments.

QUESTION

How is the receipt of the $3,000 from the Friends of John
Doolittle Committee reported by CRAMAC?

CONCLUSION

CRAMAC will report the $3,000 as an increase to cash on
Schedule G. CRAMAC should also explain on Schedule G the
circumstances surrounding the receipt.

_____________________

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

James Bratt
Page 2


ANALYSIS

The Act does not require committees to enter into loan
agreements. Loan agreements are discretionary.

The facts presented in your letter indicate that the
payments were intended to be a loan and not a monetary
contribution to the Friends of John Doolittle Committee.
During our telephone conversation of July 18, 1988, you advised
me that there was a misunderstanding between CRAMAC and the
Friends of John Doolittle Committee regarding the nature of the
contribution. I advised you to report the repayment as an
increase to cash on Schedule C of CRAMAC's Form 420 and provide
an explanation of the facts as stated in your letter of June
19, 1988.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662 if
you have any additional questions concerning this matter.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

DMG:MAK:kmt

June 19, 1988
Sacramento, CA

Fair Political Practices Commission

Subject: Request for Assistance
California Republican Assembly Membership
Action Committee # 771233

In September of 1984 I took over the reporting for the CRAMAC
account. In October of 1984 two payments were made to the
'Friends of John Doolittle' (810026), one for a special
mailing of $1,183 and the second for $1,817 for his campaign.
The payments were made conditionally, that is that if
Mr. Doolittle won his election he would return the funds.
A loan agreement was drafted but never signed.

I was advised, at the time, by the prior treasurer that since
an agreement was signed that the payment should be reported
as contributions and corrected at a future date when a signed
agreement or the money returned.

On June 17, 1988, a check for $3,00 was received by CRAMAC
from the 'Friends of John Doolittle'.

How do I report the receipt of the $3,000 and what corrections
must be made to the reports of CRAMAC?

Your speedy reply is appreciated as the reporting period is
upon us for the semi-annual report.

I can be contacted by phone at:
day 916 324-3013
night " 908-9750 (recorder on after 4 rings)
5238 Butterwood Dr.
Orangevale, CA 95662

Sincerely,

James Brett, Treasurer
